2605.18529.title: AMR-SD: Asymmetric Meta-Reflective Self-Distillation for Token-Level Credit Assignment
2605.18529.paper_summary: The alignment of Large Language Models (LLMs) for complex reasoning heavily relies on Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR). However, standard algorithms like GRPO apply sequence-level rewards uniformly to all tokens, creating a severe credit-assignment bottleneck. While on-policy self-distillation attempts to resolve this by conditioning a self-teacher on privileged contexts, direct exposure to raw oracle solutions often induces over-conditioned teacher distributions, implicit answer leakage, and late-stage training collapse. To overcome these limitations, we propose Asymmetric Meta-Reflective Self-Distillation (AMR-SD). Instead of conditioning directly on raw reference traces, AMR-SD inserts a reflection bottleneck: it compresses diagnostic signals -- from verifier outcomes, peer rollouts, or reference feedback -- into concise, self-generated Socratic hints and critiques. Furthermore, we introduce Causal Information Gain (CIG) with an asymmetric, ReLU-gated threshold to translate these reflections into sparse, highly precise token-level advantage modulations. Combined with temporal annealing, this mechanism preserves the base environmental reward while filtering out distributional noise. Experiments across scientific, mathematical, and tool-use benchmarks demonstrate that AMR-SD significantly outperforms existing baselines, achieving robust long-horizon stability and successfully preventing late-stage collapse.
2605.18529.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）對於複雜推理的對齊在很大程度上依賴於可驗證獎勵的強化學習（RLVR）。然而，像GRPO這樣的標準算法將序列級獎勵均勻地應用於所有標記，造成了嚴重的信用分配瓶頸。雖然在線自我蒸餾試圖通過將自我教師條件化於特權上下文來解決這個問題，但直接接觸原始神諭解決方案往往會導致過度條件化的教師分佈、隱式答案洩漏和晚期訓練崩潰。為了克服這些限制，我們提出了不對稱元反思自我蒸餾（AMR-SD）。AMR-SD並不是直接在原始參考痕跡上進行條件化，而是插入了一個反思瓶頸：它將來自驗證者結果、同行回滾或參考反饋的診斷信號壓縮成簡明的自我生成的蘇格拉底提示和批評。此外，我們引入了因果信息增益（CIG），使用不對稱的ReLU閾值將這些反思轉化為稀疏、高度精確的標記級優勢調整。結合時間退火，這一機制在過濾分佈噪音的同時保留了基本的環境獎勵。跨科學、數學和工具使用基準的實驗表明，AMR-SD顯著超越現有基準，實現了穩健的長期穩定性並成功防止了晚期崩潰。
2605.18529.author: Zhenlin Wei et.al.
2605.18529.authors: Zhenlin Wei, Pu Jian, Yingzhuo Deng, Xiaohan Wang, Jiajun Chai, Zhexin Hu, Wei Lin, Shanbin Zhang, Guojun Yin
